payload.message: System design causes of MoE router load imbalance in inference serving

Another useful framing is to treat MoE imbalance as a joint training and serving systems problem. The router may already prefer a narrow band of experts, and then live traffic bursts, batching artifacts, or uneven expert placement across devices make those hot experts even more expensive. Once that happens, throughput and tail latency are dictated by the busiest experts rather than average utilization. Operators usually need both model-side regularization and serving-side scheduling controls to keep expert usage smooth over time.
